steps.action: Kochari (Armenian: Քոչարի, romanized: K'očari; A with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.
steps.text: Kochari (Armenian: Քոչարի, romanized: K'očari; A with weight transfer through footwork, pivot turn, hold hands, partner opposite, accent beat, grounded.

steps.action: It is performed today by Armenians, while varian
steps.text: It is performed today by Armenians, while variants are performed by Assyrians, Azerbaijanis, and Pontic Greeks.

steps.action: It is a form of circle dance.
steps.text: It is a form of circle dance.

steps.action: Each region in the Armenian Highlands had its ow
steps.text: Each region in the Armenian Highlands had its own Kochari, with its unique way of both dancing and music.

steps.action: Dancers stand abreast, holding each other's hand
steps.text: Dancers stand abreast, holding each other's hands, The tempo of the dance ranges from moderate to fast.

steps.action: Squatting and butting an imagined opponent are f
steps.text: Squatting and butting an imagined opponent are followed by high jumps.

steps.action: The dance is danced by both men and women and is
steps.text: The dance is danced by both men and women and is intended to be intimidating.

steps.action: More modern forms of Kochari have added a "tremo
steps.text: More modern forms of Kochari have added a "tremolo step", which involves shaking the whole body.

steps.action: Kochari along with tenzere has been included to
steps.text: Kochari along with tenzere has been included to the list of Intangible Cultural Heritage in Need of Urgent Safeguarding of UNESCO in November 2018 as versions of Yalli dance.

steps.action: Unlike most Pontic dances, the Kotsari is in an
steps.text: Unlike most Pontic dances, the Kotsari is in an even rhythm (24), originally danced in a closed circle.

steps.action: Գուճ (gudj or goudj) means "knee" and արի (ari)
steps.text: Գուճ (gudj or goudj) means "knee" and արի (ari) means "come".

steps.action: In Azerbaijani Turkish, "köç" means "to move" us
steps.text: In Azerbaijani Turkish, "köç" means "to move" used both as a verb and as a noun, with the latter used more in the context of nomads' travelling.

steps.action: "Köçəri" is also both an adjective and a noun, m
steps.text: "Köçəri" is also both an adjective and a noun, meaning a "nomad" and "nomadic" simultaneously.

steps.action: It spread to the eastern part of Armenia after t
steps.text: It spread to the eastern part of Armenia after the Armenian genocide.

steps.action: The Armenian Kochari has been included to the Li
steps.text: The Armenian Kochari has been included to the List of Intangible Cultural Heritage in Need of Urgent Safeguarding of UNESCO in 2017.
